name: Grilled Chicken Appetizer Roll-Ups
recipeIngredient: 8 oz Port wine or sharp cheddar cheese, softened
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the chicken breasts in half lengthwise and season with salt and pepper.
2. In a bowl, combine olive oil, lime juice, and minced garlic. Marinate the chicken for 30 minutes or longer.
3. Remove the chicken from the marinade and grill or barbecue until cooked through (10 to 12 minutes), turning once. Alternatively, sauté in a nonstick skillet for 10 to 12 minutes.
4. Divide the cheese in half; reserve one half for later.
5. To assemble each roll-up, spread a tablespoon of cheese on the lower third of a tortilla. Place a chicken strip across the tortilla and sprinkle with a small amount of green onion.
6. Fold in the sides of the tortillas and roll up tightly. Wrap in foil; the rolls can be made ahead and refrigerated.
7. To serve, warm the roll-ups on the grill or barbecue for 8 to 10 minutes, or in the oven at 350 degrees Fahrenheit for 8 to 10 minutes.
8. Meanwhile, combine the reserved softened cheese with sour cream.
9. Remove the roll-ups from foil and cut diagonally.
10. Serve with the cheese sauce for dipping or salsa.
